narrative:
Enzo Fernández operates as a Central Midfield for Argentina. He boasts an overall rating of 7.25/10, placing him in the top 2% of central midfields at this World Cup. His rating range of 6.9–7.6 reflects the current match sample size.

At this World Cup, Fernández shows strong defensive engagement with 1.846 tackles and 1.154 clearances per 90 minutes. He has outperformed his expected goals, scoring 0.231 goals per 90 minutes against an xG of 0.158 per 90 minutes. However, his 0.462 key passes per 90 minutes suggest he is not primarily a chance creator.

Fernández is a vital presence in Argentina's central midfield, contributing both defensively and with timely goals. Scouts and fans should continue to observe his impressive goal-scoring efficiency, where his actual goals exceed his expected goals. His robust club form, which accounts for 66% of his overall rating, indicates sustained quality to watch in remaining matches.
